depression.topic: Anxiety about starting a new career
depression.description: I'm feeling overwhelmed by the idea of starting my own micro press. I keep worrying that I won't be successful and that everyone will see me as a failure.
I often feel like my value is tied to my achievements. When I think about my past projects, I can't help but feel ashamed and unworthy.
When I don't hear back from someone I'm interested in, I immediately assume they don't like me. It makes me feel so lonely and down.
I find myself constantly thinking about my past failures and how they define my competence. It's hard to shake off the feeling that I'm just not good enough.
When I'm feeling stressed, I try to engage in creative projects like printing and bookbinding. It helps me escape my worries, but I still feel anxious about the future.

Delusion.description: I often think that I'm incompetent and that I can't control my life. This belief makes it hard for me to take risks or pursue new opportunities.
I can't help but compare my journey to my peers who seem to be thriving. It reinforces my feelings of being unlovable and inadequate.
